title: Tamil Mor Kuzhambu

subs.name: plant-based coconut yogurt — use unflavoured and skip extra coconut

ingredients.name: ash gourd, peeled and cubed
visual_checks: drumstick or raw banana can replace
ingredients.notes: drumstick or raw banana can replace

subs.name: 2 drumstick pods or 150 g raw banana, cubed
subs.notes: raw banana gives a slightly starchier texture

subs.name: 2 tbsp coconut milk powder blended with a little water

steps.text: Grind the coconut, cumin seeds, and green chillies with 3 tablespoons of water in a mixie to a smooth paste. Set aside. This coconut paste is the thickener and main flavour base.
Cook the ash gourd in 200 ml of salted water with turmeric over medium flame until just tender but not mushy, about 8 minutes. Do not discard the cooking water.

Add the coconut paste to the cooked ash gourd and its water. Stir and simmer on low flame for 3 minutes until the raw coconut smell cooks away. Remove from heat before the next step — the curd must not be added to boiling liquid.

Whisk the curd until completely smooth and lump-free. Once the vegetable-paste mixture is off the heat and has cooled for one minute, pour in the curd while stirring constantly. Return to a very low flame and heat just until wisps of steam rise — do not let it boil or the curd will split.

Heat oil in a small pan. Splutter mustard seeds, add dried red chillies and curry leaves and fry 30 seconds. Pour the tempering into the kuzhambu, stir gently, and add salt. Serve warm with steamed rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: ash-gourd → 2 drumstick pods or 150 g raw banana, cubed (raw banana gives a slightly starchier texture); curd → plant-based coconut yogurt — use unflavoured and skip extra coconut; coconut → 2 tbsp coconut milk powder blended with a little water
